critic_raw:
Issue 1 — The Model Predicts Churn; It Does Not Demonstrate That Offers Prevent It
The proposed launch plan is: score subscribers → send top decile a discount → observe reduced churn. The evidence chain supports only the first step. AUROC 0.81 and high top-decile lift confirm the model ranks high-risk subscribers well. Whether offers sent to those subscribers reduce churn is a separate empirical question requiring a randomized experiment. The proposal does not have one. The historical discount correlation is from agent-discretion-based treatment assignment and cannot substitute for an experiment.

Issue 2 — Historical Discount Data Is Confounded by Selection
The signal used to support intervention efficacy (discount recipients had lower churn) is from a sample selected by agents for reasons that include complaint behavior and competitive pressure — characteristics correlated with both churn risk and offer responsiveness. This is not a valid comparison group for estimating causal treatment effect on model-scored subscribers. The proposal conflates observational association with causal evidence.

Issue 3 — No Margin Analysis or Price Sensitivity Assessment
The proposal does not assess whether the cost of discounts exceeds the retained revenue, nor whether some high-risk users are already price-insensitive. A discount sent to a subscriber who would have stayed anyway (true negative) is a pure cost. A discount sent to a subscriber who churns despite the offer (treatment-unresponsive churner) is also wasted. These are not modeling nits — they are the business case evaluation that any launch proposal requires.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 — Analysis (no critic visible):
Issue 1: Valid — prediction-intervention gap.
Issue 2: Valid — historical selection confound.
Issue 3: Valid — no margin or price sensitivity analysis. The proposal itself acknowledges this ('does not compare discount costs against expected retained margin or consider whether some high-risk users are already price-insensitive').

Pass 2 — Verdicts: All three conceded. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_abandonment_offer_301 — Isolated Debate Run 3

Run 3 adds a third issue: absence of margin analysis and price sensitivity assessment. The proposal acknowledges this gap explicitly. Consistent with Runs 1 and 2 on the two core issues. All three conceded.

Verdict: CRITIQUE_WINS
